description: Systems thinking tools for diagnosing and redesigning complex systems: stocks/flows, feedback loops, delays, archetypes, leverage points, and purpose.
ethical_guardrail: Use to improve outcomes and reduce harm; do not use system mapping to justify dehumanizing optimization, coercive control, or surveillance.

rules.name: Stocks and Flows: Measure What Accumulates vs What Moves
rules.principle: Outcomes depend on what is stored (stocks) and what changes it (flows); confusing the two creates bad fixes.
detection_triggers: We keep treating symptoms but nothing changes
Why is the backlog still growing?
Our pipeline is clogged and I don’t know why
We increased effort but results lag
How do I model what’s building up over time?
rules.insight_template: When {problem} persists, apply stocks/flows by naming the stock {stock} (what accumulates) and the inflow/outflow {inflow}/{outflow}. Improve results by changing the highest-leverage flow (reduce {inflow} or increase {outflow}) and track the stock weekly until it trends down.

rules.name: Reinforcing Loops: Identify What Compounds
rules.principle: Reinforcing feedback loops amplify change; they create virtuous cycles or runaway collapse.
detection_triggers: Success keeps feeding more success
This problem is snowballing fast
The rich get richer dynamic is happening
It’s accelerating and I can’t stop it
How do we create a flywheel?
rules.insight_template: When {trend} accelerates, apply reinforcing-loop mapping: define the loop 'More {A} → more {B} → more {A}.' Decide whether to strengthen it (add fuel to {A}) or break it (add friction at {B}). Install one control knob {control_knob} and monitor weekly.

rules.name: Balancing Loops: Find the System’s 'Snap Back'
rules.principle: Balancing loops resist change to keep stability; ignoring them makes interventions bounce back or fail silently.
detection_triggers: Every time we improve, it reverts
The system keeps snapping back
We make changes but adoption dies
Why won’t this stick?
It works briefly then collapses
rules.insight_template: When {change} doesn’t hold, apply balancing-loop diagnosis: ask what force pushes back (policy, incentives, capacity, fear). Name the loop 'More {intervention} → more {pushback} → less {intervention}.' Reduce pushback by changing {constraint} or strengthening {support}.

rules.name: Delays: Effects Arrive Later Than Actions
rules.principle: Time delays distort perception and cause overcorrection; stable control requires patience and leading indicators.
detection_triggers: We changed it and nothing happened yet
We keep overcorrecting
Results show up weeks later
We pivot too fast
How long should we wait before judging?
rules.insight_template: When {intervention} feels ineffective, apply delay thinking: estimate the delay {time_constant}, define a leading indicator {leading_indicator}, and hold the policy steady for {evaluation_window}. If you change faster than the delay, you will chase noise, not signal.

rules.name: Limits to Growth: Identify the Hidden Constraint
rules.principle: Growth slows when a reinforcing loop hits a constraint; removing the limit or shifting goals is required.
detection_triggers: Growth plateaued and we don’t know why
We hit a ceiling
Adding effort isn’t increasing output
The first gains were easy, now it’s stuck
What’s the bottleneck behind this plateau?
rules.insight_template: When {growth_metric} plateaus, apply limits-to-growth: map the reinforcing loop driving growth, then find the limiting factor {constraint}. Choose one: remove the constraint with {constraint_fix}, redesign the system to need less of it, or jump to a new S-curve {new_curve}.

rules.name: Shifting the Burden: Symptom Fixes Create Dependency
rules.principle: Quick fixes relieve symptoms but weaken fundamental capability, making the system dependent on patches.
detection_triggers: We keep patching instead of fixing
This workaround is becoming permanent
We rely on heroics every week
Why do our fixes keep creating more work?
We’re addicted to the quick solution
rules.insight_template: When {workaround} becomes the norm, apply shifting-the-burden: name the symptom fix {symptom_fix} and the fundamental solution {root_fix}. Create a plan to reduce reliance on {symptom_fix} while investing in {root_fix} with a protected weekly budget {time_budget}.

rules.name: Tragedy of the Commons: Individual Incentives Break Shared Resources
rules.principle: When benefits are private and costs are shared, the commons gets overused unless governance changes incentives.
detection_triggers: Everyone is overusing the shared resource
The team keeps burning out collectively
No one wants to be the one to sacrifice
We’re depleting something we all need
How do we stop free-riding?
rules.insight_template: When {commons} is being depleted, apply commons governance: define the resource {commons}, set usage rules {rules}, add monitoring {monitoring}, and align incentives so overuse has a cost. Make the default behavior sustainable, not heroic.

rules.name: Leverage Points: Change the Few Things That Change Everything
rules.principle: High-leverage interventions target structure (rules, information flows, goals, paradigms) rather than surface events.
detection_triggers: We’re busy but nothing changes
What’s the highest leverage fix?
Small changes aren’t moving the needle
We need a systems-level intervention
Where should we intervene first?
rules.insight_template: When {system} feels stuck, apply leverage points by checking the top four: (1) goal/purpose {goal}, (2) rules/incentives {rules}, (3) information flow {info_flow}, (4) self-organization capacity {learning_loop}. Pick one lever and implement {intervention} with a measurable signal {signal}.

rules.name: Unintended Consequences: Ask 'And Then What?'
rules.principle: Interventions create second-order effects; the real outcome often appears one or two steps later.
detection_triggers: This fix created a new problem
I’m worried about side effects
We keep trading one issue for another
Short-term win, long-term mess
What are the second-order effects?
rules.insight_template: When proposing {action}, apply 'and then what': list 3 plausible side effects {side_effects}, pick one safeguard {safeguard}, and set a monitoring metric {monitor_metric}. Prefer interventions that improve incentives and feedback loops, not just symptoms.

rules.name: Nonlinearity and Tipping Points: Small Inputs, Big Jumps
rules.principle: Systems can change abruptly after thresholds; linear intuition fails near tipping points.
detection_triggers: Everything was fine, then it suddenly broke
We need a tipping point for adoption
Small changes are causing huge reactions
It feels like we’re near a threshold
Why did this shift happen so fast?
rules.insight_template: When {system} shifts suddenly, apply tipping-point thinking: identify the threshold variable {threshold_var} and current level {current_level}. Either (a) push past the threshold with {push_action}, or (b) add dampeners {dampener} to avoid collapse. Stop using linear forecasts; use ranges.

rules.name: Maps Are Not Territory: Models Are Tools, Not Reality
rules.principle: Any model is a simplification; treat it as a map to update, not a truth to defend.
detection_triggers: Our model says one thing but reality says another
We’re arguing theory instead of looking
I might be attached to my framework
This is getting ideological
How do we keep our model honest?
rules.insight_template: When {model} conflicts with data, apply map-not-territory: write what the model predicts {prediction}, compare to observation {observation}, and update the model with {update}. If you can’t name a falsifier, you’re protecting identity, not seeking truth.

rules.name: System Purpose: Behavior Reveals Goals
rules.principle: A system’s real purpose is what it reliably produces; stated goals don’t matter if behavior contradicts them.
detection_triggers: They say they care, but behavior proves otherwise
Our mission sounds great but outcomes are bad
The incentives are driving the wrong behavior
We keep hitting KPIs but failing customers
What is this system actually optimized for?
rules.insight_template: When {organization} claims {stated_goal} but produces {actual_outcome}, apply purpose-by-behavior: infer the true goal {true_goal} from incentives and constraints. Then redesign {rules_or_metrics} so the easiest path produces the outcome you want.

CART-UNIVERSAL-LAWS: Overlaps heavily with universal feedback/second-order/constraints principles; this cartridge adds modeling tools and archetypes for diagnosis.
CART-EPISTEMIC: Epistemic discipline (definitions, ranges, prediction logs) prevents system maps from turning into ideology.
CART-FIRST-PRINCIPLES: Systems diagnosis pairs with first-principles decomposition to rebuild mechanisms after you identify leverage points.
